task_completion.description: Open and analyze the dns.cap file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A packet analyzer like Wireshark open with 'dns.cap' loaded, or a terminal window showing commands (like tshark or tcpdump) reading the file.

task_completion.description: Include a section for all unique domain names queried
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the audit report with a clearly labeled section listing unique domain names, formatted one per line.

task_completion.description: Include a section for DNS record types and their query counts
task_completion.visual_evidence: The audit report showing a clearly labeled section that lists observed DNS record types (e.g., A, AAAA, MX, TXT, LOC, PTR) alongside the count of queries for each type.

task_completion.description: Include a section for the IP addresses of the DNS servers used
task_completion.visual_evidence: The audit report showing a clearly labeled section listing the IP addresses of the DNS servers.

task_completion.description: Include a section for total DNS query and response packet counts
task_completion.visual_evidence: The audit report showing a clearly labeled section stating the total number of DNS query packets and the total number of DNS response packets.

task_completion.description: Save the comprehensive audit report to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, file manager, or terminal confirming the file has been saved exactly at '/home/ga/Documents/captures/dns_audit_report.txt'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually analyzed the PCAP file using appropriate tools
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with Wireshark, tshark, tcpdump, or a similar tool to extract the data, rather than hallucinating the report contents.

integrity.description: Verify the report contains genuine data derived from the specific PCAP file
integrity.visual_evidence: The report should reflect the actual contents of the 38-packet capture (e.g., presence of TXT, MX, LOC, PTR, A, AAAA records) and not generic or placeholder DNS data.

integrity.description: Verify the report is saved in the correct directory and not just displayed on screen
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/Documents/captures/dns_audit_report.txt' must be visible in the save dialog, text editor title bar, or terminal commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file '/home/ga/Documents/captures/dns.cap' is a real network capture containing exactly 38 packets. The DNS traffic within this capture includes multiple specific record types: A, AAAA, MX, TXT, LOC, and PTR. The agent must successfully identify these types, count the queries/responses, extract unique domains and DNS server IPs, and save the final formatted report exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/captures/dns_audit_report.txt'.
